description: Order pizza, browse menus, optimize deals, and track delivery from your terminal — with a local SQLite store that powers reorder, price comparison, and deal stacking no other Domino's tool offers.

auth_env_var_specs.name: DOMINOS_USERNAME
auth_env_var_specs.description: Domino's account email or phone consumed by 'auth login' to obtain a bearer token. Read-only commands work without.

auth_env_var_specs.name: DOMINOS_PASSWORD
auth_env_var_specs.description: Domino's account password consumed by 'auth login' to obtain a bearer token.

auth_env_var_specs.name: DOMINOS_TOKEN
auth_env_var_specs.description: Bearer token populated automatically by 'auth login' and read by authenticated commands. Do not set manually.

novel_features.name: Named order templates
novel_features.description: Save your usual order — store, address, items, toppings, payment ref — and replay it with one command.
novel_features.rationale: Requires SQLite-persisted templates that survive across sessions; no existing wrapper persists named orders.
novel_features.example: dominos-pp-cli template save friday-night --from-cart && dominos-pp-cli template order friday-night --eta-watch --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or user wants a one-command repeat of a known-good order without rebuilding the cart from scratch.

novel_features.name: Smart reorder with substitution
novel_features.description: Replay your last order against today's menu; FTS-substitute items the menu rotated out so the order still goes through.
novel_features.rationale: Joins synced order history with the current menu's FTS5 index — neither piece exists in any wrapper alone.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a saved-order-style replay must survive Domino's menu rotation; substitution avoids the 'this item is no longer available' failure.

novel_features.name: Deal optimizer
novel_features.description: Cross-reference your cart against every available deal (incl. loyalty-exclusive) and report the lowest-priced combination.
novel_features.rationale: Domino's CheckDraftDeal is per-deal; combining N deals against one cart requires local fan-out and ranking.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before placing an order whenever the user cares about price; the flag enumerates 2-3-deal combinations the website never surfaces.

novel_features.name: Cross-store price comparison
novel_features.description: Same cart priced at every nearby store; rank by total including delivery fee.
novel_features.rationale: Multi-store fan-out depends on the local menu cache; community wrappers query one store at a time.
novel_features.example: dominos-pp-cli compare-prices --address "421 N 63rd St" --city "Seattle WA" --items S_PIZPH,S_LAVA --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when latency-or-price tradeoffs across nearby stores matter (delivery fee + wait time can offset a cheaper menu).

novel_features.name: Multi-store wait-time scoreboard
novel_features.description: Pull CartEtaMinutes for every store in radius and rank by ETA — the unique GraphQL BFF op every other wrapper ignores.
novel_features.rationale: CartEtaMinutes is reverse-engineered and absent from every existing wrapper; combining it across stores is novel.
novel_features.example: dominos-pp-cli stores wait --address "421 N 63rd St" --city "Seattle WA" --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when busy-hour delivery decisions need accurate wait estimates rather than a phone call to the store.

novel_features.name: Live delivery tracker with polling
novel_features.description: Stream Domino's tracker stages — placed → prep → bake → quality check → out → delivered — until the order arrives.
novel_features.rationale: node-dominos has single-shot tracker only; status-transition streaming requires interval polling and state diffing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or user wants to know precisely when a placed order changes stage without holding open a browser tab.

novel_features.name: One-shot agent order
novel_features.description: Replay a template, validate, price, place (with --confirm), and tail the tracker — all in one command emitting a final JSON envelope.
novel_features.rationale: Composes existing primitives into the single agent-shaped affordance Ace persona explicitly asked for.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or automation wants to trigger an order and exit cleanly with structured `{order_id, eta_min, total, tracker_phone}` output.

novel_features.name: Cart-shaped deal eligibility
novel_features.description: List which advertised deals actually apply to your current cart and explain why each non-matching one fails.
novel_features.rationale: DealsList exposes per-deal predicates the UI hides; locally evaluating them against the cart is unique to the local data layer.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user is hunting for a coupon and needs to understand the predicate gap, not just whether 'a deal' applies.

novel_features.name: Spending analytics
novel_features.description: Aggregate your synced order history into spending totals, item frequency, favorite stores, and average order value.
novel_features.rationale: Order history is auth-gated and ephemeral on the website; persisting it locally is the only way to compute multi-period rollups.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when 'how much have I spent on pizza this quarter' or 'what are my top 3 items' is the question. Powered entirely by the local SQLite order history.
